title: Quiz: Complex Numbers
description: Test your understanding of complex numbers and their role in quantum computing

questions.question: What is the value of i⁴, where i is the imaginary unit?
questions.explanation: Powers of i cycle every 4: i¹=i, i²=-1, i³=-i, i⁴=1. Since i⁴ = i² × i² = (-1)(-1) = 1.

questions.question: For the complex number z = 3 - 4i, what is its complex conjugate z*?
questions.explanation: The complex conjugate is found by negating the imaginary part. For z = 3 - 4i, we have z* = 3 + 4i.

questions.question: What is the modulus |z| of the complex number z = 5 + 12i?
questions.explanation: |z| = √(a² + b²) = √(5² + 12²) = √(25 + 144) = √169 = 13. This is a 5-12-13 Pythagorean triple.

questions.question: Calculate (2 + 3i)(1 - i). What is the result?
For any complex number z, the product z × z* always equals a real number.
questions.explanation: True! For z = a + bi, we have z × z* = (a + bi)(a - bi) = a² + b² = |z|², which is always a non-negative real number.

questions.question: In quantum computing, if |ψ⟩ = α|0⟩ + β|1⟩, the probability of measuring |0⟩ is simply α.
questions.explanation: False! The probability is |α|² (modulus squared), not α itself. Since α can be complex, we need to take the absolute value squared to get a real probability value.

questions.question: On the complex plane (Argand diagram), which axis represents the imaginary part?
questions.explanation: On the complex plane, the horizontal axis represents the real part and the vertical axis represents the imaginary part. This is analogous to x and y coordinates.

questions.question: Which of the following statements about the imaginary unit i are correct? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: a) Correct: i² = -1 by definition. b) Correct: i = √(-1) is the definition. c) Incorrect: i³ = i² × i = -1 × i = -i, not i. d) Correct: |i| = |0 + 1i| = √(0² + 1²) = 1.
